creator: george@m5p.com
text: This problem still exists in December 2016.  I've found a less insecure workaround.  Precondition: you are running gpg-agent.  Before starting thunderbird, decrypt any file from the command line with gpg2.  (Or after starting thunderbird, navigate away from the message you can't decrypt; run gpg2; then navigate back to the message of interest.)  Thunderbird will now be able to decrypt messages using the key whose passphrase you entered for gpg2.
